key_observations: Clear V-shaped recovery pattern: volume collapsed from 16.9km (week 1) to 6.3km (week 5), then rebuilt to 15.7km (week 6)—suggests planned deload or injury recovery followed by successful return to training

HR data reveals improving aerobic efficiency: baseline 158 bpm at 15.7km (week 6) increased slightly to 163 bpm at lower 6.8km volume (week 7), indicating elevated intensity work post-deload rather than fitness loss
Pace consistency maintained throughout (5:39–6:14 min/km for runs, excluding football), suggesting aerobic base preserved despite 3-week low-volume period
warnings: 5-week gap between May 13 and May 29 activities with dramatic volume drop—investigate underlying cause (injury, illness, schedule disruption) to prevent recurrence
Sudden return to 3-run week (week 6) after extended low volume carries injury risk; monitor for overuse signals in subsequent weeks
recommendations: Maintain current 3-run-per-week structure with 15–18km total; you've demonstrated solid recovery capacity but avoid rapid volume spikes beyond 10% weekly increase
Leverage elevated HR trend (158→163) to structure one dedicated tempo/threshold session weekly; your recent football session (avg HR 158) shows readiness for structured intensity work
Restore HR monitoring consistency across all runs—last 6 weeks show sporadic data; continuous tracking will reveal overtraining signals earlier and optimize pacing strategy

weeks_analysis.assessment: Strong start: 2 runs averaging 6:06–6:14 pace. Baseline fitness established at solid aerobic level.
Single 13.67km run at 6:14 pace. Reduced frequency suggests early fatigue or schedule constraint; maintained pace indicates no fitness drop.
12-day gap precedes this week; dramatic 43% volume reduction. Deload/recovery phase or unplanned disruption; pace held at 6:11.
Continued low volume (single 7.23km run). Apparent extended recovery block; maintain caution during rebuild phase.
Nadir: minimum volume week (6.32km single run). Baseline during recovery; pace improved to 5:47, suggesting freshness despite low volume.
Strong recovery: 3-run week with 15.7km total (near week 1 levels). HR data onset here (avg 158) reveals quality aerobic work; successful rebuild achieved.
Tapering week: single 6.85km run at elevated HR (163 vs 158 baseline). Suggests deliberate reduction with intensity maintenance; likely pre-event preparation.
